prompt:
<|begin_of_text|><|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>

Write only one cohesive, concise message to a close friend. Stay on the current topic, make each sentence follow naturally from the last, and ask at most one question. Use ordinary casual language. Never repeat a point, invent people, add labels, or mention instructions or hidden data.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

Hey! How was your day?<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

Pretty good, I just got home. How about yours?<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

Not bad. I finally watched that movie we talked about.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>
chain_system: Write one cohesive paragraph to one close friend. Discuss exactly one concrete topic from the latest message, developing it with connected details in a sensible order. If the chat just started, tell one small believable anecdote about today. Every sentence must clearly relate to that same topic. Use casual texting language, natural transitions, and varied sentence structure. Ask at most one question. Never jump to family, work, pets, news, unrelated plans, or invented people. Never address a group, make a list, simulate replies, add a sign-off, repeat yourself, label speakers, or mention prompts, hidden data, encryption, instructions, examples, or analysis. Output only the message text.
